verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: An Index of the months of each year, and of the days, and of the Indictions, and of the Consulates, and of the Governors in Alexandria, and of all the Epacts, and of those [days] which are named ‘of the Gods,’ and the reason [any Letter] was not sent, and the returns from exile—from the Festal Letters of Pope Athanasius.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: The Festal Letters of Athanasius, Bishop of Alexandria, which he sent year by year, to the several cities and all the provinces subject to him; that is, from Pentapolis, and on to Libya, Ammoniaca, the greater and the lesser Oasis, Egypt, and Augustamnica, with the Heptanomis of the upper and middle Thebais; [commencing] from the 44th year of the Diocletian Era, in which the Paschal Festival was on xvi Pharmuthi; xviii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xviii Moon; when Alexander, his predecessor, having departed this life on xxii Pharmuthi, he [Athan.] succeeded him after the Paschal festival on xiv Pauni, Indict. i, Januarius and Justus being Consuls, the governor Zenius of Italy being the Præfect of Egypt, Epact xxv; Gods, i.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xi Pharmuthi; viii.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xxii Moon; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Constantinus Aug. viii, Constantinus Cæs.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: IV; the same governor Zenius being Præfect of Egypt; Indict. ii; Epact vi; Gods, ii.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: This was the first Letter he [Athan.] sent; for he was ordained Bishop in the preceding year after the Paschal feast, Alexander, as is known, having despatched one for that year, before he was released from life.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: This was in the 45th of the Diocletian era.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxiv Pharmuthi; xiii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xv Moon; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Gallicianus, Symmachus; the governor Magninianus the Cappadocian being Præfect of Egypt; Indict. iii; Epact xvii; Gods, iii.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year he went through the Thebais.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvi Pharmuthi; xviii Moon; iii Id.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Annius Bassus, Ablavius; the governor Hyginus of Italy, Præfect of Egypt; Epact xxviii; Indict. iv.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He sent this Letter while journeying on his return from the Imperial Court.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: For in this year he went to the Imperial Court to the Emperor Constantine the Great, having been summoned before him, on account of an accusation his enemies made, that he had been appointed when too young.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He appeared, was thought worthy of favour and honour, and returned when the fast was half finished.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvii Pharmuthi; xx Moon; iv Non.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Apr.; Epact ix; Gods, vi; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Pacatianus, Hilarianus; the same governor Hyginus, Præfect of Egypt; Indict. v.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year he went through Pentapolis, and was in Ammoniaca.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xx Pharmuthi; xv Moon; xvii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Optatus, Paulinus; the same governor Paternus Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year he went through the lower country.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In it he was summoned to a Synod, his enemies having previously devised mischief against him in Cæsarea of Palestine; but becoming aware of the conspiracy, he excused himself from attending.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xiv Pharmuthi; xx Moon; iii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Indict. viii; Epact xii; Gods, ii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Constantius, Albinus; the same governor Paternus, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxiii Pharmuthi, xx Moon; xiv Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; Indict. ix; Epact xxiii; Gods, iv; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Nepotianus, Facundus; the governor Philagrius, the Cappadocian, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year he went to that Synod of his enemies which was assembled at Tyre.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Now he journeyed from this place on xvii Epiphi, but when a discovery was made of the plot against him, he removed thence and fled in an open boat to Constantinople.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Arriving there on ii Athyr, after eight days he presented himself before the Emperor Constantine, and spoke plainly.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But his enemies, by various secret devices, influenced the Emperor, who suddenly condemned him to exile, and he set out on the tenth of Athyr to Gaul, to Constans Cæsar, the son of Augustus.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: On this account he wrote no Festal Letter.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on viii Pharmuthi; xvi Moon; iv Non.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Indict. x; Epact iv; Gods, v; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Felicianus, Titianus; the governor Philagrius, the Cappadocian, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He was in Treviri of Gaul, and on this account was unable to write a Festal Letter.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxx Phamenoth; vii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xix Moon, Indict. xi; Epact xv; Gods, vi; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ursus, Polemius; the governor Theodorus, of Heliopolis, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Constantine having died on xxvii Pachon, Athanasius, now liberated, returned from Gaul triumphantly on xxvii Athyr.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, too, there were many events.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Antony, the great leader, came to Alexandria, and though he remained there only two days, shewed himself wonderful in many things, and healed many.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He went away on the third of Messori.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xx Pharmuthi; xx Moon; xvii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; Epact xxvi; Gods, vii; Indict. xii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Constantius II, Constans I; the governor Philagrius, the Cappadocian, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, again, there were many tumults.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: On the xxii Phamenoth he was pursued in the night, and the next day he fled from the Church of Theonas, after he had baptized many.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Then, four days after, Gregorius the Cappadocian entered the city as Bishop.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xiv Pharmuthi; xv Moon; iii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Epact vii; Gods, ii; Indict. xiii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Acyndinus, Proclus; the same governor Philagrius, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Gregorius continued his acts of violence, and therefore [Ath.] wrote no Festal Letter.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: The Arians proclaimed [Easter] on xxvii Phamenoth, and were much ridiculed on account of this error.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Then altering it in the middle of the fast, they kept it with us on iv Pharmuthi, as above.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He [Athanasius] gave notice of it to the presbyters of Alexandria in a short note, not being able to send a letter as usual, on account of his flight and the treachery.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxiv Pharmuthi; xvi Moon; xiii.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; Epact xviii; Gods, iii; Indict. xiv; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Marcellinus, Probinus; the governor Longinus, of Nicæa, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Augustamnica was separated.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: On account of Gregorius continuing in the city, and exercising violence, although this illness commenced, the Pope did not write a Festal Letter even this time.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvi Pharmuthi; xx Moon; iii Id.

verses.text: Ap.; Epact xxix; Gods, iv; Indict. xv; Coss.

verses.text: Constantius III, Constans II; the governor Longinus of Nicæa, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Because Gregorius was in the city, [though] severely ill, the Pope was unable to send [any Letter].

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on i Pharmuthi; xv Moon; vi Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Epact xi; Gods, v; Indict. i; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Placidus, Romulus; the same governor Longinus, of Nicæa, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year the Synod of Sardica was held; and when the Arians had arrived, they returned to Philippopolis, for Philagrius gave them this advice there.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In truth, they were blamed everywhere, and were even anathematised by the Church of Rome, and having written a recantation to Pope Athanasius, Ursacius and Valens were put to shame.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: There was an agreement made at Sardica respecting Easter, and a decree was issued to be binding for fifty years, which the Romans and Alexandrians everywhere announced in the usual manner.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Again he [Athan.] wrote a Festal Letter.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xx Pharmuthi; xix Moon; xvii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; Epact xxi; Gods, vi[i], Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Leontius, Sallustius; the governor Palladius, of Italy, Præfect of Egypt; Indict. ii.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Being at Naissus on his return from the Synod, he there celebrated Easter.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Of this Easter-day he gave notice in few words to the presbyters of Alexandria, but he was unable to do so to the country.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xii Pharmuthi; xviii Moon; vii.

verses.text: Amantius, Albinus; the governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Having travelled to Aquileia, he kept Easter there.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Of this Easter-day, he gave notice in few words to the presbyters of Alexandria, but not to the country.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on iv Pharmuthi; xxi Moon; iii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: III; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Gregorius having died on the second of Epiphi, he returned from Rome and Italy, and entered the city and the Church.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Moreover he was thought worthy of a grand reception, for on the xxiv Paophi, the people and all those in authority met him a hundred miles distant, and he continued in honour.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He had already sent the Festal Letter for this year, in few words, to the presbyters.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvii Pharmuthi xv.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Rufinus, Eusebius; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He wrote this Letter while residing here in Alexandria, giving notice of some things which he had not been able to do before.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on vii Pharmuthi; xviii Moon; iii Non.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But because the Romans refused, for they said they held a tradition from the Apostle Peter not to pass the twenty-sixth day of Pharmuthi, nor..the thirtieth of Phamenoth, xxi Moon,………………, vii Kal.

verses.text: Limenius, Catullinus; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.text: He sent this also while residing in Alexandria.

verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xiii Pharmuthi; xix Moon, the second hour; vi Id.

verses.text: Sergius, Nigrianus; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Constans was slain by Magnentius, and Constantius held the empire alone; then he wrote to the Pope [Athan.], telling him to fear nothing because of the death of Constans, but to confide in him as he had done in Constans while living.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter day was on v Pharmuthi; Moon xviii; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Epact ix; Gods, i; Indict. ix; the Consulship after that of Sergius and Nigrianus; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, again Præfect of Egypt.

verses.text: V, Constantius Cæsar I; the same governor Nestorius of Gaza, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Gallus was proclaimed Cæsar, and his name changed into Constantius.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvi Pharmuthi; xxi Moon; iii Id.

verses.text: VI, Constantius Cæsar II; the governor Sebastianus of Thrace, præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Serapion, Bishop of Thmuis, and Triadelphus of Nicion, and the presbyters Petrus and Astricius, with others, were sent to the emperor Constantius, through fear of mischief from the Arians.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: They returned, having effected nothing.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Montanus, Silentiarius of the Palace, [was sent]…against [the] Bishop, but, a tumult having been excited, he retired, having failed to effect anything.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on i Pharmuthi; xvii Moon; vi Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Arbetion, Lollianus; the governor Maximus the Elder of Nicæa, Prefect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Diogenes, the Secretary of the Emperor, entered with the design of seizing the Bishop.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But he, too, having raged in vain, went away quietly.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xii Pharmuthi; xvii Moon; vii Id.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; Epact iv; Gods, i; Indict. xiv, Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Constantius Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: VIII, Julianus Cæsar I; the same governor Maximus the Elder of Nicæa, Præfect of Egypt, who was succeeded by Cataphronius of Byblus.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Syrianus Dux, having excited a tumult in the Church on the thirteenth of Mechir, on the fourteenth at night entered Theonas with his soldiers; but he was unable to capture [Athanasius], for he escaped in a miraculous manner.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxvii Phamenoth; xvii Moon; x Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap., Epact xv; Gods, ii; Indict. xv; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: IX, Julianus Cæsar II; the same governor Cataphronius, of Byblus, Præfect of Egypt, to whom succeeded Parnassius.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Then Georgius entered on the thirtieth of Mechir, and acted with excessive violence.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But Athanasius, the Bishop, had fled, and was sought for in the city with much oppression, many being in danger on this account.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Therefore no Festal Letter was written.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvii Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.text: Ap.; xvii Moon; Epact xxvi; Gods, iii; Indict. i; Coss.

verses.text: Athanasius, the Bishop, lay concealed in the city of Alexandria.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But Georgius left on the fifth of Paophi being driven away by the multitude.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: On this account, neither this year was the Pope able to send a Festal Letter.

verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on ix Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: This Præfect and Artemius Dux, having entered a private house and a small cell, in search of Athanasius the Bishop, bitterly tortured Eudæmonis, a perpetual virgin.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Taurus, Florentius; the same governor Faustinus, Præfect of Egypt, who was succeeded by Gerontius the Armenian.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He was unable to send [a Letter].

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Constantius died, and Julianus holding the empire alone, there was a cessation of the persecution against the Orthodox.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: For commands were issued everywhere from the emperor Julianus, that the Orthodox ecclesiastics who had been persecuted in the time of Constantius should be let alone.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on v Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xxv Moon; Epact x; Gods, i; Indict. v; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mamertinus, Nevitta; the same governor Gerontius, who was succeeded by Olympus of Tarsus.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, in Mechir, Athanasius the Bishop returned to the Church, after his flight, by the command of Julianus Augustus, who pardoned all the Bishops and Clergy in exile, as was before said.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: This year, then, he wrote [a Letter].

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxv Pharmuthi; xii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xx Moon; Epact xxi; Gods, ii; Indict. vi; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Julianus Augustus IV, Sallustius; the same governor Olympus, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Pythiodorus Trico of Thebes, a Philosopher, brought a decree of Julianus on the twenty-seventh of Paophi, and set it in action against the Bishop first, and uttered many threats.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: So he [Athan.] left the city at once, and went up to the Thebais.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: And when after eight months Julianus died, and his death was announced, Athanasius returned secretly by night to Alexandria.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Then on the eighth of Thoth, he embarked at the Eastern Hierapolis, and met the emperor Jovian, by whom he was dismissed with honour.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He sent this festal Letter to all the country, while being driven by persecution from Memphis to the Thebais, and it was delivered as usual.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on ix Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xvi Moon; Epact iii; Gods, iv; Indict. vii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Jovianus Aug., Varronianus; the governor Aerius, of Damascus, Præfect; who was succeeded by Maximus of Rapheotis, and he again by Flavianus the Illyrian.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, the Pope returned to Alexandria and the Church on the twenty-fifth of Mechir.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He sent the Festal Letter, according to custom, from Antioch to all the Bishops in all the province.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on i Pharmuthi; v[i] Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xix Moon; Epact xiv; Gods, v; Indict. viii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Valentinianus Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: I, Valens Aug.; the same Flavianus, the Illyrian, being governor.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: We received the Cæsareum; but again, the Pope being persecuted with accusations, withdrew to the garden of the new river.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: But a few days after, Barasides, the notary, came to him with the Præfect and obtained an entrance for him into the Church.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Then, an earthquake happening on the twenty-seventh of Epiphi, the sea returned from the East, and destroyed many persons, and much damage was caused.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxi Pharmuthi; xvi Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xx Moon; Epact xxv; Gods, vi; Indict. ix; in the first year of the Consulship of Gratianus, the son of Augustus, and Daglaiphus; the same governor Flavianus, Præfect.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: On the twenty-seventh of Epiphi, the heathen made an attack, and the Cæsareum was burnt and consequently many of the citizens suffered great distress, while the authors of the calamity were condemned and exiled.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: After this, Proclianus the Macedonian, became chief.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on vi Pharmuthi; Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xvi Moon; Epact vi; Gods, vii; Indict. x; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Lupicinus, Jovinus; the same Proclianus being governor, who was succeeded by Tatianus of Lycia.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, when Lucius had attempted an entrance on the twenty-sixth of Thoth, and lay concealed by night in a house on the side of the enclosure of the Church; and when Tatianus the Præfect and Trajanus Dux brought him out, he left the city, and was rescued in a wonderful manner, while the multitude sought to kill him.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year he [Ath.] wrote, forming a Canon of the Holy Scriptures.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxv Pharmuthi; xii Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xvi Moon; Epact xvii; Gods, ii; Indict. xi; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Valentinianus Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: II, Valens Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: II; the same governor Tatianus, Præfect.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He [Athan.] began to build anew the Cæsareum, on the 6th of Pachon, having been honoured with an imperial command by Trajanus Dux.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: He also discovered the incendiaries, and immediately cleared away the rubbish of the burnt ruins, and restored the edifice in the month Pachon.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xvii Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xv Moon; Epact xxviii; Gods, iii; Indict. xii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Valentinianus (son of Augustus) I, Victor; the same Tatianus being governor.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: The Pope began to build that Church in Mendidium which bears his name, on the twenty-fifth of the month Thoth, at the beginning of the eighty-fifth year of the Diocletian Era.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on ii Pharmuthi; v Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xv Moon; Epact ix; Gods, iv; Indict. xiii; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Valentinianus Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: III, Valens Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: III; the same Tatianus being governor, who was succeeded by Olympius Palladius, of Samosata.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: The Pope finished the Church, called after his name, at the close of the eighty-sixth year of the Diocletian Era; in which also he celebrated the dedication, on the fourteenth of Mesori.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xxii Pharmuthi; xv Kal.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Mai; xvi Moon; Epact xx; Gods, v; Indict. xiv; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Gratianus Aug.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: II, Probus; the same Palladius being governor; who was succeeded as Præfect of Egypt by Ælius Palladius, of Palestine, who was called Cyrus.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on xiii Pharmuthi; vi Id.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xix Moon; Epact i; Gods, vii, Indict. xv; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Modestus, Arintheus; the same Ælius Palladius the governor, called Cyrus, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: In this year, Easter-day was on v Pharmuthi; Prid.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Ap.; xxi Moon; Epact xii; Gods, i; Indict. i; Coss.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: Valentinianus IV, Valens IV; the same governor Ælius Palladius, Præfect of Egypt.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: At the close of this year, on the seventh of Pachon, he [Athan.] departed this life in a wonderful manner.

verses.book: The Festal Letters, and their Index.
verses.text: The end of the heads of the Festal Letters of holy Athanasius, Bishop of Alexandria.
